Calculation.Text: SetProperty ( "level" ; 3 ) & SetProperty ( "caller" ; "Title") & SetProperty ( "server_mode" ; "Preview" ) & SetProperty ( "package_dir" ; FAT Package @ Media::Export Package Sub Path ) & SetProperty ( "package_friendly_id" ; FAT Package @ Media::JSON Friendly id ) & SetProperty ( "package_type_name" ; FAT Package @ Media::Package type name )

StyledText.Data: <<FAT Title::Reference title>> | Number of media files: <<ƒ:GetAsText ( ValueCount (List of PrimaryKey))>> | <<Container original filename>>

Calculation.Text: "View all media with subtitles in title"

ScriptReference.name: Go all Block instances in Title

Calculation.Text: Case ( FAT Title::gViewTranslatedLanguage ; TF Subtitle::Localisation code = $$localisation_code ; TF Subtitle::Localisation code = FAT Media::Localisation code )
"Enter the subtitle text for this video timecode"

LayoutObject.name: PI navigation

Calculation.Text: FAT Media::Localisation code = FAT Title::Original localisation code

StyledText.Data: Native video This media subtitle will be exported as a separate VTT file named: <<Container exported filename no extension>>.<<FAT Title::Original localisation code>>.vtt

CharacterStyle.Text: <<Container exported filename no extension>>.<<FAT Title::Original localisation code>>.vtt

StyledText.Data: Funkis video This media subtitle will be exported together with other subtitles in the file named: cmt_subtitles_<<FAT Title::Original localisation code>>.json

Import VTT or SRT subtitle file You can import a VTT or SRT subtitle file. Please note: 1. The files must be encoded in UTF-8 and with LF. 2. The import file format does not affect the export file format.

CharacterStyle.Text: Import VTT or SRT subtitle file
The files must be encoded in UTF-8 and with LF. 2. T

StyledText.Data: Filename: <<Container original filename>> • Localisation code: <<Localisation code>>
Import XML You can import subtitles from a XML subtitle file from old Funkis Tool.
When “yes” is ticked, the video in this block will be played using the browser’s native video player. This will enable features like full-screen.
Use the Sonix tool to create new subtitles
This media file has localised versions.
Copy all subtitle texts to clipboard

Calculation.Text: "Export subtitles to this media in VTT format."

StyledText.Data: Created by: <<CreatedBy>>. • <<CreationTimestamp>> |. Last modified by: <<ModifiedBy>> • <<Container modified>>
